summary: REQ-6: Dashboards & Communications - All 37 backend tests passed (100%). All frontend UI elements verified working. Executive Dashboard shows 6 metric cards + 7 widgets. Lead Management has full CRUD + status pipeline + conversion. Feedback has CRUD + auto-escalation + resolution.

issues: Console warning: Missing `Description` or `aria-describedby={undefined}` for {DialogContent} - accessibility improvement recommended

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/reports/executive-dashboard returns 200
Backend: Executive dashboard has today stats (ip_checkins, op_checkins, revenue, collected, appointments)
Backend: Executive dashboard has active patients (ip_patients, op_patients, queue_waiting, queue_in_consultation)
Backend: Executive dashboard has monthly revenue (revenue, collected)
Backend: Executive dashboard has room occupancy (total, occupied, available, occupancy_rate)
Backend: Executive dashboard has doctor_performance list
Backend: Executive dashboard has lead stats (total, new)
Backend: POST /api/leads creates new lead with status=new
Backend: GET /api/leads returns list of leads
Backend: GET /api/leads?status=new filters by status
Backend: PUT /api/leads/{id} updates lead details
Backend: PUT /api/leads/{id} returns 404 for non-existent lead
Backend: PUT /api/leads/{id}/status?status=contacted changes status
Backend: PUT /api/leads/{id}/status?status=follow_up changes status
Backend: PUT /api/leads/{id}/status with invalid status returns 400
Backend: PUT /api/leads/{id}/status returns 404 for non-existent lead
Backend: POST /api/leads/{id}/convert creates patient with auto PID
Backend: Lead status changes to 'converted' after conversion
Backend: Patient created from lead has correct data (name, phone, email)
Backend: POST /api/leads/{id}/convert returns 400 for already converted lead
Backend: POST /api/leads/{id}/convert returns 404 for non-existent lead
Backend: DELETE /api/leads/{id} deletes lead
Backend: DELETE /api/leads/{id} returns 404 for non-existent lead
Backend: POST /api/feedback with rating >= 3 creates non-escalated feedback
Backend: GET /api/feedback returns list of feedback
Backend: GET /api/feedback/summary returns total, average_rating, escalations, unresolved
Backend: DELETE /api/feedback/{id} deletes feedback
Backend: DELETE /api/feedback/{id} returns 404 for non-existent feedback
Backend: POST /api/feedback with rating <= 2 auto-creates escalation
Backend: POST /api/feedback with rating = 2 creates escalation
Backend: GET /api/feedback?escalation_only=true returns only escalated feedback
Backend: PUT /api/feedback/{id}/resolve marks escalation as resolved
Backend: GET /api/feedback/summary counts escalations correctly
Frontend: Executive Dashboard page loads with all 6 metric cards
Frontend: Dashboard has Live Queue, Appointments, Active Patients widgets
Frontend: Dashboard has Patient Distribution chart (Recharts)
Frontend: Dashboard has Doctor Performance section
Frontend: Dashboard has Revenue Summary and Quick Stats
Frontend: Lead Management page loads with status filter cards (new, contacted, follow_up, converted, lost)
Frontend: Leads table shows lead data with status dropdown and action buttons
Frontend: Add Lead dialog has all fields (name, phone, email, source, inquiry_type, follow_up_date, notes)
Frontend: Lead status can be changed via dropdown
Frontend: Feedback page loads with summary cards (Total, Avg Rating, Escalations, Unresolved)
Frontend: Google Review CTA with Copy Link button present
Frontend: Feedback list shows items with escalation tags and Resolve button
Frontend: Add Feedback dialog has patient select, star rating, source, feedback text
Frontend: Escalation can be resolved with notes
Frontend: Escalations filter button toggles escalation-only view
critical_code_review_comments: DialogContent components missing aria-describedby for accessibility - add DialogDescription or aria-describedby prop (minor, non-blocking)
Google Review URL is placeholder (https://g.page/r/YOUR_GOOGLE_PLACE_ID/review) - user needs to replace with actual Google business link
seed_data_creation: Test lead 'Priya M' (status: new, source: whatsapp) exists. Test feedback with rating 2 (escalated, now resolved) exists.
context_for_next_testing_agent: REQ-6 Dashboards & Communications fully functional. Executive Dashboard API returns today/monthly stats, room occupancy, doctor performance, lead counts. Lead CRUD working with status pipeline (new→contacted→follow_up→converted→lost). Lead conversion creates patient with auto PID (TAH-XXXX format). Feedback CRUD working with auto-escalation for ratings ≤2. Escalation resolution working. Google Review URL is placeholder.

details.metrics: today (ip/op checkins, revenue, appointments), active (ip/op patients, queue status), monthly (revenue, collected), rooms (occupancy), doctor_performance, leads (total, new)

details.create: POST /api/leads with name, phone, email, source, inquiry_type, notes, follow_up_date
details.conversion: POST /api/leads/{id}/convert creates patient with auto PID

details.create: POST /api/feedback with patient_id, patient_name, rating, feedback_text, source
details.summary: GET /api/feedback/summary returns total, average_rating, escalations, unresolved

details.metric_cards: OP Today, IP Today, Today Revenue, Month Revenue, Room Occupancy, New Leads
details.widgets: Live Queue, Appointments Today, Active Patients, Patient Distribution Chart, Doctor Performance, Revenue Summary, Quick Stats

details.status_filters: Clickable cards for new, contacted, follow_up, converted, lost
details.table: Shows lead name, phone, source, inquiry, status, follow-up date, notes, actions
details.actions: Status dropdown, Convert button, Edit button, Delete button
details.dialogs: Add Lead and Edit Lead dialogs with all fields

details.summary_cards: Total Feedback, Average Rating (with stars), Total Escalations, Unresolved
details.google_review_cta: Copy Link button for Google Review URL
details.feedback_list: Shows patient name, star rating, source, escalation tag, resolve button
details.dialogs: Add Feedback dialog with patient select, star rating, source, feedback text
